description_long: This is a vintage Singer treadle sewing machine mounted on a sturdy rectangular wooden table with a dark finish. The sewing machine itself is made of black cast metal with metallic accents and features a prominent handwheel on the right side. The base is constructed from ornate black cast iron, including a foot pedal and intricate latticework, providing both support and a decorative touch. The table includes a small side drawer for storing sewing accessories. The machine's body displays classic curves and a metallic needle arm, with visible mechanical components. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, reflecting early 20th-century craftsmanship.
description: A vintage Singer treadle sewing machine on a dark wooden table with ornate black cast iron legs and a side drawer. The machine features a prominent handwheel and classic metallic detailing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Singer logo, treadle pedal, and the main sewing machine body with the handwheel on the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the circular handwheel, intricate cast iron base, and the mechanical linkage beneath the table.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the sewing machine, the side drawer, and the full width of the table.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the needle arm, presser foot area, and the cast iron pedal structure.
attribute_brand_logo: The word 'SINGER' is visibly cut out in white on the black cast iron base, centered below the table surface.
